title: Tell me about yourself
content: Craft a compelling 2-minute pitch covering your background, key achievements, and why you're interested in Google.
expectedAnswer: Structure: Present → Past → Future. Start with current role, highlight relevant achievements, connect to why Google excites you.
sampleAnswer: I'm a software engineer with 3 years of experience building scalable distributed systems at [Company]. I led the redesign of our real-time data pipeline that reduced latency by 40% and handles 1M events/sec. I'm drawn to Google because of the scale of problems you solve and the opportunity to impact billions of users.
tips: Show genuine interest in Google's mission

title: Explain OOP Principles
content: Explain the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming with real-world examples.
expectedAnswer: Cover Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, and Polymorphism with code examples.
sampleAnswer: The four pillars are: 1) Encapsulation - bundling data and methods, controlling access via private/public. 2) Abstraction - hiding complexity, exposing only necessary interfaces. 3) Inheritance - creating hierarchies for code reuse. 4) Polymorphism - same interface, different implementations.
followUp: When would you prefer composition over inheritance?

title: Design a URL Shortener
content: Design a URL shortening service like bit.ly that can handle millions of URLs.
expectedAnswer: Cover requirements, API design, database schema, encoding algorithm, caching, and scaling strategies.
sampleAnswer: Requirements: shorten URLs, redirect, analytics. Use base62 encoding with counter/hash. Store in NoSQL (DynamoDB). Cache hot URLs in Redis. Use CDN for redirects. Handle 100M URLs/day with horizontal scaling.
followUp: How would you handle custom aliases?

title: What are your salary expectations?
content: How to handle salary expectation questions professionally.
expectedAnswer: Research market rates, provide a range, emphasize total compensation.
sampleAnswer: Based on my research and experience level, I'm looking at a total compensation in the range of $X-$Y, but I'm flexible and more focused on the role's growth potential and impact.

title: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager
content: Amazon tests 'Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit' principle with this question.
expectedAnswer: Use STAR method. Show respectful disagreement backed by data, and commitment to the final decision.
sampleAnswer: Situation: My manager wanted to use a monolithic architecture for a new service. Task: I believed microservices would be better for our scale. Action: I prepared a comparison document with performance projections and presented it in a team meeting. Result: We adopted a hybrid approach that reduced deployment time by 60%.
followUp: What would you do if your manager still disagreed?
How did this affect your relationship?

title: Design an e-commerce recommendation system
content: Design a product recommendation engine for Amazon-scale e-commerce.
expectedAnswer: Cover collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, real-time vs batch processing, A/B testing framework.
sampleAnswer: Use collaborative filtering (user-item matrix) + content-based (product features). Batch compute with Spark, real-time with Kafka streams. Store in Redis for low-latency serving. A/B test with feature flags.
tips: Cover both online and offline components
followUp: How to handle the cold start problem?

title: Why Microsoft?
content: Explain your motivation for joining Microsoft specifically.
expectedAnswer: Connect personal values with Microsoft's mission to empower every person and organization.
sampleAnswer: I'm passionate about Microsoft's mission to empower every person and organization to achieve more. The scale of Azure's infrastructure challenges excites me, and I admire the growth mindset culture that Satya Nadella has fostered.
followUp: Which Microsoft product would you improve?
How does our mission align with your career goals?

title: Design Facebook News Feed
content: Design the News Feed system for a social media platform with billions of users.
expectedAnswer: Cover feed generation (push vs pull), ranking algorithm, caching strategy, and real-time updates.
sampleAnswer: Hybrid push/pull model: push for users with few friends, pull for celebrities. Rank using ML model considering recency, engagement, relationship. Cache pre-computed feeds in Redis. Use WebSocket for real-time updates.
